title: Read the file and output to console

source.submodule: File handling - File opening modes, file handling functions

topicTags: File handling - File opening modes, file handling functions

content.problemStatement:
Let us now read the contents of this file and output the same to the console.

Review the sample syntax below
```cpp
    string filename = "/sample_file.txt";
    ofstream myFile(filename);
    int number;
    
    // Read and display the numbers from the file
    while (myFile >> number) {
        cout > number`: This reads an integer from the file. 
   - The loop continues as long as `myFile` successfully reads an integer and returns a value that is not `NULL`.
   - Inside the loop, the program prints each read integer using `cout << number << "\n";`.
- After reading and displaying all the integers, the `myFile.close();` statement closes the file.\
It is important to close the file after you're done with it to free up system resources.

### Task
Read the syntax given above carefully. \
You have an **`input.txt`** file stored at the location **`/mnt/codechef/`**. \
Update the code given in the IDE to output using `cout` all the contents of the file.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq N \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ over all testcases does not exceed $2\cdot 10^3$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of user inputs.
- Each user input consists of 2 lines of input 
  - 1st line is the value 1 / 2 / 3 indicating `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
  - 2nd line has the `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
content.outputFormat: Output as space separated values on a single line - `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
